data.content:
### Define AnimationTiming Type with JSDoc Comments for Auto-Generated Documentation in TypeScript Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/README.md Demonstrates how to document TypeScript types using JSDoc comments that can be automatically extracted for Storybook documentation. The comment describes the purpose of the type, which defines easing function options for animations. This pattern enables auto-generation of arg descriptions in the Storybook API documentation. ```typescript /** The type of easing function to use for animations */ export type AnimationTiming = 'ease' | 'ease-in' | 'ease-out' | 'ease-in-out' | 'linear'; ``` -------------------------------- ### Configure Recharts Line Chart Animation in React Source: https://context7.com/recharts/recharts/llms.txt This React component demonstrates how to control animation properties for a Line chart using Recharts. It includes setting `animationDuration`, `animationEasing`, `animationBegin`, and handling `onAnimationStart`/`onAnimationEnd` callbacks. A button is provided to replay the animation by changing the chart's key. ```jsx import React, { useState } from 'react'; import { LineChart, Line, XAxis, YAxis, CartesianGrid, Tooltip, ResponsiveContainer } from 'recharts'; const data = [ { name: 'Page A', uv: 4000 }, { name: 'Page B', uv: 3000 }, { name: 'Page C', uv: 2000 }, { name: 'Page D', uv: 2780 }, { name: 'Page E', uv: 1890 }, ]; function AnimationExample() { const [animationKey, setAnimationKey] = useState(0); return (
console.log('Animation started')} onAnimationEnd={() => console.log('Animation ended')} />
); } export default AnimationExample; ``` -------------------------------- ### Apply Custom Domain with Data Overflow for Recharts YAxis Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/wiki/Axis-domains-and-ticks This Recharts YAxis configuration defines a custom numerical domain using `domain={[1e23, 2e27]}`. By setting `allowDataOverflow` to `true`, the axis will strictly adhere to the specified domain, effectively cutting off data points that fall outside this range, such as the Sun's mass in the solar system example. ```jsx ``` -------------------------------- ### Format YAxis Ticks with Custom Unit Conversion in Recharts JSX Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/wiki/Axis-domains-and-ticks Demonstrates how to customize Y-axis tick labels in Recharts using the tickFormatter prop. The example converts kilogram values to yottagrams by dividing by 1e24 and formatting to two decimal places. This allows for flexible unit transformations and custom number formatting on chart axes. ```jsx function kgToYottagram(value: number): string { // the data is defined in kg const yottagram = value / 1e24; return `${yottagram.toFixed(2)}`; } // ... ``` -------------------------------- ### Import Trapezoid with Storybook Documentation Setup Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/shapes/Trapezoid.mdx Imports necessary Storybook addon modules and Trapezoid component stories for documentation and interactive testing. This setup enables the Meta, Canvas, and Controls components to display component API documentation and interactive controls in Storybook. ```javascript import { Meta, Canvas, Controls } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'; import * as ComponentStories from './Trapezoid.stories'; ``` -------------------------------- ### Render Basic Recharts Bar Chart with Default Axes Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/wiki/Axis-domains-and-ticks This JSX snippet renders a Recharts BarChart displaying solar system data. It uses an `XAxis` for names and a `YAxis` for mass, demonstrating default axis behavior where the Y-axis range automatically covers all data, making smaller values invisible due to the large scale difference. ```jsx ``` -------------------------------- ### Set Logarithmic Scale and Custom Domain for Recharts YAxis Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/wiki/Axis-domains-and-ticks This Recharts YAxis configuration applies a logarithmic scale using `scale="log"` to better visualize data with a wide range of values, like planetary masses. A custom domain `domain={[1e22, 3e30]}` is also specified, which is often required for the logarithmic scale to function correctly. ```jsx ``` -------------------------------- ### Define Solar System Data Array for Recharts Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/wiki/Axis-domains-and-ticks This JavaScript array defines the data points for celestial bodies in a solar system, including properties like name, orbital distance, radius, mass, and a fill color. It serves as input for Recharts components to visualize planetary data, highlighting objects with significantly different values. ```jsx const solarSystem = [ { name: 'Sun', orbitalDistanceKm: 0, radiusKm: 696340, massKg: 1.989e30, fill: '#ffa700', }, { name: 'Mercury', orbitalDistanceKm: 5.791e7, radiusKm: 2439.7, massKg: 3.3e23, fill: '#1a1a1a', }, // ... rest of the planets ] ``` -------------------------------- ### Access Exited Docker Container for Debugging (Shell) Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/test-vr/README.md These shell commands allow you to inspect the contents of an exited Docker container. First, commit the container's final state to a new image, then run an interactive bash session from that image to explore its file system. ```sh docker commit debug docker run -it --rm debug bash ``` -------------------------------- ### Configure Recharts YAxis as Category Type Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/wiki/Axis-domains-and-ticks This Recharts YAxis configuration sets the `type` prop to 'category'. This treats numerical data as discrete categorical values, displaying each unique mass value as a separate tick rather than a continuous spectrum, which can lead to unsorted and unrounded ticks. ```jsx ``` -------------------------------- ### Create Initial MDX File with ArgTypes Table for Storybook Component Documentation Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/README.md Shows the minimal MDX file setup required when documenting a new component in Storybook. This imports the component stories and renders an ArgTypes table sorted with required props first. This serves as the foundation for component documentation before adding detailed descriptions to individual props. ```mdx import * as ComponentStories from './Component.stories'; ``` -------------------------------- ### Build Scatter Chart with Multi-dimensional Data in Recharts Source: https://context7.com/recharts/recharts/llms.txt Create a scatter plot visualization with multiple data series, Z-axis scaling for bubble sizes, and custom cell colors. Supports multiple scatter groups with different shapes and styling options. Uses CartesianGrid, Tooltip, and Legend components for enhanced interactivity and data clarity. ```jsx import React from 'react'; import { ScatterChart, Scatter, XAxis, YAxis, ZAxis, CartesianGrid, Tooltip, Legend, ResponsiveContainer, Cell } from 'recharts'; const data01 = [ { x: 100, y: 200, z: 200 }, { x: 120, y: 100, z: 260 }, { x: 170, y: 300, z: 400 }, { x: 140, y: 250, z: 280 }, { x: 150, y: 400, z: 500 }, { x: 110, y: 280, z: 200 } ]; const data02 = [ { x: 300, y: 300, z: 200 }, { x: 400, y: 500, z: 260 }, { x: 200, y: 700, z: 400 }, { x: 340, y: 350, z: 280 }, { x: 560, y: 500, z: 500 } ]; const COLORS = ['#0088FE', '#00C49F', '#FFBB28', '#FF8042', '#8884d8']; function ScatterChartExample() { return ( {data01.map((entry, index) => ( ))} ); } export default ScatterChartExample; ``` -------------------------------- ### Build Sunburst Chart with Recharts for Hierarchical Data Source: https://context7.com/recharts/recharts/llms.txt Creates a hierarchical radial visualization showing parent-child relationships in a circular layout with nested rings. Displays multi-level categorical data with color-coded segments representing different branches. Dependencies include React and Recharts SunburstChart component. Input requires nested data structure with name, value, fill, and children properties. ```jsx import React from 'react'; import { SunburstChart, ResponsiveContainer } from 'recharts'; const data = { name: 'Root', children: [ { name: 'Sales', value: 4000, fill: '#8884d8', children: [ { name: 'Q1', value: 1200, fill: '#8884d8' }, { name: 'Q2', value: 1000, fill: '#83a6ed' }, { name: 'Q3', value: 900, fill: '#8dd1e1' }, { name: 'Q4', value: 900, fill: '#82ca9d' }, ], }, { name: 'Marketing', value: 3000, fill: '#82ca9d', children: [ { name: 'Digital', value: 1800, fill: '#82ca9d' }, { name: 'Print', value: 700, fill: '#a4de6c' }, { name: 'Events', value: 500, fill: '#d0ed57' }, ], }, { name: 'Engineering', value: 5000, fill: '#ffc658', children: [ { name: 'Frontend', value: 2000, fill: '#ffc658' }, { name: 'Backend', value: 2200, fill: '#ffb347' }, { name: 'DevOps', value: 800, fill: '#ff9c33' }, ], }, ], }; function SunburstExample() { return ( ); } export default SunburstExample; ``` -------------------------------- ### Render basic Recharts Curve component Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/shapes/Curve.mdx This JSX snippet illustrates the basic usage of the Recharts Curve component, which serves as a foundational SVG shape. It can be used standalone or within a `` component, accepting various properties (represented by `crossProps`) to define its appearance and behavior. ```jsx ``` -------------------------------- ### Build Commands for Development and Production with Pre-rendering Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/www/SSG_README.md Commands to run the Recharts documentation site in different modes. Development mode runs as a normal SPA without pre-rendering for faster iteration. Production build includes full pre-rendering of all routes, sitemap generation, and validation scripts. ```bash npm start # Development mode - no pre-rendering npm run build # Production build - with pre-rendering and validation ``` -------------------------------- ### Import PieChart and Storybook Components Source: https://github.com/recharts/recharts/blob/main/storybook/stories/API/chart/PieChart.mdx Imports necessary Storybook documentation components and PieChart story definitions for rendering interactive documentation. Demonstrates the typical setup for documenting Recharts components using Storybook's addon-docs. ```javascript import { Canvas, Meta, ArgTypes } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'; import * as PieChartStories from './PieChart.stories'; ```
